speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: This is the tenor of the Emperor’s writ:

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: That since the common men are now in action

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: And that the legions now in Gallia are

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Full weak to undertake our wars against

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: The fall’n-off Britons, that we do incite

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: The gentry to this business. He creates

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Lucius proconsul; and to you the tribunes

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: For this immediate levy, he commends

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: His absolute commission. Long live Caesar!

speaker: TRIBUNE
text: Is Lucius general of the forces?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Which I have spoke of, whereunto your levy

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Must be supplyant. The words of your commission

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Will tie you to the numbers and the time
